selected_important_turn.text: It seems we both need the water so If you want 2 water, I would need all the food and firewood as well.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is a high-leverage, escalatory counteroffer that turns a contested resource into an all-or-nothing demand (you get 2 waters -> I take everything else). It shapes the distribution of all remaining items and strongly frames the negotiation as zero-sum, influencing whether the parties will move toward agreement or further positional bargaining.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is overly rigid and punitive: conditioning the opponent getting 2 waters on handing over all food and firewood closes off integrative options (e.g., splitting other goods, side payments, trades over quantities). That rigidity increases conflict risk and reduces trust; it also ignores the opponent's repeated stated need for water and the opportunity to explore creative trades.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the demand and move from an all-or-nothing stance to a problem-solving offer: acknowledge their need, propose specific, limited tradeoffs (e.g., what combination of food/firewood they'd accept for 2 waters), or propose alternative splits that leave both with enough resources. Ask a clarifying question to uncover flexibility (e.g., which items are least valuable to them) instead of insisting on everything.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by opening integrative trade space and enabling mutually beneficial splits)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces stalemate risk by creating concrete, smaller concessions)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less adversarial tone, more cooperative framing, builds trust)

value2reason.Low: I have insulated sleeping bags so I don't need as much Firewood.
value2reason.Medium: I have a larger crowd that is coming camping with me
value2reason.High: The stream doesn't have fresh water

value2reason.Low: I have sleeping bags and warm clothes as well
value2reason.Medium: I fear I do not have enouhg
value2reason.High: I forgot to bring an so I need it to survive

dialogue.text: Ireall would like the water as I forgot to bring it and would not be able to survive without it
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can trade more food but I need a bit more water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I do need the water as well, I can  trade more firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: No I want need at least two packages of water.  I can give you two packages of food and all my firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Sorr I can give you 1 water only or I would not have enough for myself.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can give you more food if you like but I really need the water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: It seems we both need the water so If you want 2 water, I would need all the food and firewood as well.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need 2 waters for survival. If you agree, I’ll split the rest evenly—2 food and 1 firewood for you, 1 food and 2 firewood for me. Let’s find a fair balance.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need 2 waters for my group. If you agree, I’ll give you 2 food and 1 firewood, and I’ll take 1 food and 2 firewood. That way we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for water. How about this: I’ll give you 2 waters, and in return, you can choose 2 food and 1 firewood. That leaves me with 1 food and 2 firewood. It’s fair and ensures we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm happy with the offer. Here's the final split: I get 2 waters, 2 foods, and 1 firewood. You get 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewoods. Let's head out!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
